options: All lines at the same time
challenges.explanation: Python reads and runs code from top to bottom, one line at a time. So Line 1 prints first, then Line 2, then Line 3 — in exactly the order they appear.

options:
Starting up!
NameError: name 'oops' is not defined
challenges.explanation: Python runs line by line from the top. The first print succeeds and outputs 'Starting up!'. Then Python hits the second line and finds that 'oops' is not a defined variable, so it raises a NameError and stops immediately. The third print never runs.

challenges.prompt: This code has a bug. What is wrong?
options: The variable 'message' is used before it is defined — the assignment must come first
The string needs to use single quotes instead of double quotes
Python cannot store text in a variable called 'message'
challenges.explanation: Python reads top to bottom. When it reaches print(message) on line 1, the variable 'message' has not been created yet — that only happens on line 2. Moving the assignment above the print call fixes the NameError.

challenges.prompt: Put these lines in the right order so the code runs without errors and prints the favorite color.
challenges.explanation: Python must see the variable 'color' defined before it can use it in print(). Defining the variable first (line 1) then printing it (line 2) follows Python's top-to-bottom execution order and avoids a NameError.
